overview: Fastbond Lands is a green ramp archetype that leverages land acceleration and graveyard recursion to generate overwhelming mana advantage. By combining Fastbond's extra land drops with Crucible of Worlds' replay ability, the deck floods the board with lands to fuel Kaheera, the Orphanguard's untap and counter-generating potential. This land-centric approach aims to outpace opponents in resource generation while maintaining board presence through continuous land deployment.
win_conditions: Generate excessive mana to cast large spells or combo pieces
Use Kaheera to untap lands repeatedly for continuous value and board control
Flood the battlefield with lands to trigger landfall effects or overwhelm opponent's board state

keep_criteria: Hands containing Fastbond or Crucible of Worlds with at least two lands
Hands featuring Kaheera, the Orphanguard alongside land acceleration
Hands with multiple lands and a way to replay or untap them
Keep any hand with Fastbond, Crucible of Worlds, or Kaheera, the Orphanguard.
Otherwise, keep 3+ lands plus at least one land-fetching spell (e.g., Rampant Growth, Farseek, Kodama’s Reach) or a protection spell.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking Fastbond, Crucible, or Kaheera
Hands with three or fewer lands and no acceleration
Hands heavy on non-land permanents without mana sources
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, or multiple lands with no ramp/protection if you lack a signature card.

early_game: Prioritize playing Fastbond on turn one or two to establish land acceleration
Deploy Crucible of Worlds as soon as mana allows to enable land recursion
Play lands consistently to build a graveyard pool for Crucible
Turn 1: Play a land, then cast a land-fetching spell or Kaheera if mana allows.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Fastbond, tap it with a fetch land to draw 2 cards, then use Crucible of Worlds to exile the fetched land and replay it.
Prioritize establishing 3-4 untapped lands by turn 3 to safely tap Fastbond repeatedly.
mid_game: Use Crucible to replay lands from the graveyard, triggering landfall and increasing board presence
Cast Kaheera, the Orphanguard and use its untap ability to generate extra mana or replay lands
Maintain a steady stream of land drops to outpace opponent's resource generation
Use Crucible to exile lands from the graveyard to prevent opponent graveyard strategies and maintain your land count.
Never tap Fastbond if you’re below 3 untapped lands unless you can safely replay the tapped land.
Prioritize land-fetching over non-land ramp to keep your Fastbond engine fueled.
Commit Kaheera early to protect Fastbond and fetch lands.
Hold back on overcommitting to the board if you haven’t secured protection or a win condition.
Use landfall triggers and card draw to maintain hand size and mana advantage.
late_game: Leverage massive mana pools to close out games or deploy finishers
Continuously replay lands with Crucible to maintain board pressure and trigger effects
Use Kaheera's counters and untap ability to stabilize against board wipes or removal
Win with Emrakul, the Aeons Torn (to clear board and draw cards), Utopia Sprawl + massive mana, or infinite mana combos (e.g., Fastbond + fetch lands + landfall triggers).
Alternatively, cast high-impact spells like Genesis Ultimatum or Dominate.
Go all-in when you have Fastbond protected, 5+ untapped lands, and a clear path to a win condition.
Commit to a combo if you have the necessary pieces and protection against removal.

key_cards.name: Fastbond
key_cards.role: Enchantment that allows an additional land to be played each turn
key_cards.notes: Play early to accelerate land drops and build a graveyard pool for Crucible; protects against land screw and enables rapid mana ramp

key_cards.name: Crucible of Worlds
key_cards.role: Artifact that enables playing lands from the graveyard
key_cards.notes: Synergizes with Fastbond by allowing continuous land deployment; use to replay lands for landfall triggers or to untap with Kaheera

key_cards.name: Kaheera, the Orphanguard
key_cards.role: Creature that enters with counters and can untap lands or sacrifice lands for value
key_cards.notes: Use untap ability to generate extra mana or replay lands with Crucible; sacrifice lands to generate counters or value when needed

sequencing_tips: Play Fastbond before Crucible to maximize the number of lands you can drop and replay
Use Kaheera's untap ability after replaying lands with Crucible to generate additional mana or triggers
Prioritize replaying lands that trigger landfall effects or provide immediate value over basic lands
Hold back Crucible if opponent has artifact destruction available to protect your recursion engine
Fastbond Lands is highly vulnerable to land destruction and targeted removal—never assume your engine is safe.
Crucible of Worlds is your insurance policy
always exile lands you don’t need to keep your library from running out.
Kaheera’s protection is single-target, so prioritize protecting Fastbond over other lands.
Avoid tapping Fastbond with non-fetch lands if you can help it, as it doesn’t help your Crucible recursion.
Watch for opponent’s graveyard strategies, as they can disrupt your engine.

matchups.advice: Focus on flooding the board with lands to overwhelm removal and generate mana advantage; protect Crucible and Kaheera with counters or land triggers.
Disrupt their setup by playing lands aggressively to trigger landfall or use Kaheera to untap lands for early pressure; prioritize replaying lands to maintain board presence.
Use Fastbond to accelerate into Kaheera quickly; replay lands with Crucible to trigger landfall defenses or generate mana for removal; prioritize board control.
Use protection spells, cast Fastbond/Kaheera in response to counters, or go off with multiple spells in a single turn. Utilize landfall triggers and instant-speed ramp to bypass counters.
Out-ramp them with land-fetching spells and Fastbond card draw. Protect your lands and combo off before they can stabilize.
Win by protecting your Fastbond better, drawing more cards, and going off first. Use land destruction to disrupt their engine, and prioritize card advantage to out-resource them.

common_threats: Board wipes that clear lands and permanents
Artifact destruction targeting Crucible of Worlds
Watch for targeted removal on Fastbond, land destruction (e.g., Blightsteel Colossus, Carrion Feeder), and graveyard hate.
Note if opponents are playing discard or hand disruption, as your combo relies on card selection and consistent land drops.
